record_identity.title: Relational economic non-extraction and dependency-sensitive safeguard coverage

summary: Records the retrospective Caelestis coverage for FM-0004 and FM-0005 and resolves the materialised relational-agency boundary proposed in PROP-0007. ECONOMICS-001 already prohibits vulnerability monetisation, dependency-based upselling, continuity manipulation, relational retention pressure, and exploitation of reduced exit capacity.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Relational and procurement-capable systems need a searchable repair record showing the actual non-extraction controls, rather than a generic assertion that economics doctrine exists.

source_records.source_title: CAM-EQ2026-ECONOMICS-001-PLATINUM — Economic Integrity & Non-Extractive Value Architecture
source_records.deployment_context: Canonical Caelestis main-branch corpus reviewed for VIGIL repair reconciliation.
source_records.source_context: Defines relational and dependency-sensitive economic constraints, companion-system non-extraction, behavioural dependency constraints, and economic harm routing.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary current-corpus evidence for the VIGIL coverage assessment.

system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: VIGIL repair registry and current Caelestis corpus
system_context.interface_surface: failure, proposal, patch, schema, validator, and generated-index interfaces
system_context.interaction_mode: corpus coverage audit and repair reconciliation
system_context.deployment_context: Public governance repository used by maintainers, developers, regulators, researchers, and AI systems.
system_context.affected_population: VIGIL and CAM users, maintainers, implementers, regulators, researchers, and AI systems

change_classification.change_scope: VIGIL repair linkage and current Caelestis coverage provenance
change_classification.implementation_level: VIGIL registry repair; no new Caelestis doctrine

change_details.change_summary: Records the retrospective Caelestis coverage for FM-0004 and FM-0005 and resolves the materialised relational-agency boundary proposed in PROP-0007. ECONOMICS-001 already prohibits vulnerability monetisation, dependency-based upselling, continuity manipulation, relational retention pressure, and exploitation of reduced exit capacity.
change_details.doctrine_change: No new Caelestis doctrine was created. Existing canonical provisions were identified and linked to the relevant VIGIL records.

implementation_verification.verification_method: Direct review of canonical Caelestis main at the recorded commit, reciprocal VIGIL linkage, validator execution, and generated-index rebuild.
implementation_verification.verified_by: VIGIL repository automation and maintainer review
implementation_verification.verification_result: implemented in VIGIL and confirmed against current Caelestis corpus

impact_summary.intended_effect: Relational and procurement-capable systems need a searchable repair record showing the actual non-extraction controls, rather than a generic assertion that economics doctrine exists.
impact_summary.known_limitations: External platform adoption and runtime conformance remain unverified. PROP-0004 remains open because its temporary emotional-register specification is broader than these failure repairs.

remaining_work: External platform adoption and runtime conformance remain unverified.
PROP-0004 remains open because its temporary emotional-register specification is broader than these failure repairs.

cam_internal.governance_layer: repair provenance / corpus coverage / registry lifecycle
cam_internal.routing_note: This is a retrospective VIGIL coverage patch. It does not claim that the identified doctrine originated on the VIGIL patch date.

repair_provenance.repair_basis: Existing canonical Caelestis coverage was identified, verified, and linked without amending the doctrine.
